title: Tribes seek to overturn Corps permit for controversial Alaska gold-dredging project
summary: Yereth Rosen Alaska Beacon Three Alaska Native tribes have filed a lawsuit to overturn the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers permit granted to a company seeking to dredge for gold in a sensitive wetland area near Nome. The tribes, in their complaint filed on April 10 in U.S. District Court in Anchorage, said the Corps had erred by issuing a wetlands-fill permit...

title: AST Criminal Trespass: Fairbanks [AK25036900]
summary: On 04/19/2025, at approximately 1446 hours, Alaska State Troopers were dispatched to a break-in in Fairbanks. Troopers' investigation determined that Russell Hill committed the crimes of Criminal Trespass and Violation of conditions of release. Russell was arrested and transported to FCC.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: AST Medical Assist: Glennallen [AK25036373]
summary: On 04/18/2025 at approximately 1045, Alaska State Trooper received a report of a Snow machine crash in the area of Lake Louise. Troopers and VPSOs responded. Troopers ensured scene safety and conducted landing zone operations. The injured party was airlifted by LifeMed due to injuries.

title: AST Fail to Stop/DUI/MIW4: Fairbanks [AK25036729]
summary: On April 19, 2025, at approximately 2:37 AM, an Alaska State Trooper initiated a traffic stop on a brown Ford pickup truck after locking its speed through calibrated radar at 71 MPH in a posted 40 MPH zone. The driver failed to stop as directed by the officer, the driver accelerated, reaching speeds of over 100 MPH. The vehicle eventually came to a stop on Illinois Street near the GVEA Building. The driver, identified as Airon Baumgartner, was arrested and taken to the Fairbanks Correctional Center on charges of Failure to Stop in the Second Degree, Driving Under the Influence (DUI), and Misconduct Involving a Weapon in the Fourth Degree.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

entities.address_detail: a brown Ford pickup truck after locking its speed through calibrated radar at 71 MPH in a posted 40 MPH zone

title: AST Promoting Contraband/ MICS 4: Palmer [AK25036700]
summary: On April 19, 2025, at approximately 00:34 hours, AST responded to Matsu Pretrial for an individual bringing a controlled substance into the correctional facility. The investigation revealed that Jamie Gerst, 51, of Wasilla, had concealed a controlled substance on her body while entering the facility. Jamie was charged with promoting contraband in the first degree and misconduct involving a controlled substance in the fourth degree. She was held with no bail.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

misconduct involving a controlled substance in the fourth degree

title: AST Warrant Arrest: Wasilla [AK25035651]
summary: On 4/18/2025 at approximately 2230 hours, AST responded to a residence on Rainbow Park Circle in Wasilla for follow-up. At the residence, AST contacted Jamie Gerst, 51, of Wasilla. A check in APSIN revealed Jamie had an outstanding $10,000 warrant for her arrest for the original charge of Theft 2 x2 and Theft of an Access device. Jamie was arrested, transported, and remanded at Matsu Pretrial, where she was held for her $10,000 bail.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

entities.address_detail: Rainbow Park Circle in Wasilla for follow-up
